summary: Exceptional soundboard capture (with minimal audience patches) delivers a smooth 70s groove, highlighted by stellar Jack Straw, Here Comes Sunshine, Playin' in the Band and an inspired Truckin→Other One.
review: The SBD version of this unknown‑date show offers crystal‑clear mix and stereo depth, only marred by occasional splices on “Bertha” and a missing board for “Black Peter.” Musically the band is in top form – Jerry’s tone smokes, Phil’s bass prowls, Keith’s Rhodes shines, and the setlist flows effortlessly from upbeat numbers like Jack Straw to expansive jams such as The Other One through Bobby McGee.

ai_rating.rationale: All three reviewers praise the performance and most aspects of the recording; minor tape‑splices and missing board segments keep it from being perfect but overall rating is high.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Exceptional fidelity and stereo after Brown Eyed Women, strong kick drum presence; occasional splices/skips in “Bertha,” slight tinny high‑end distortion on patched audience sections, missing board for “Black Peter.”

show_quality.setlist_flow: Smooth 70s groove with seamless segues (e.g., China→Rider) and dynamic jams; the band hits every peak in sync, creating an engaging, flowing concert experience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Guitar tone praised throughout – “smokes” on Jack Straw, bright on Here Comes Sunshine, turned up on They Love Each Other, and strong vocal harmonies.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass work highlighted as huge in the mix during The Other One and kills it on Playin' in the Band; overall bass lines described as perfect.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboards noted for disappearing during part of Playin' jam but later delivering perfect Rhodes on He's Gone and splattering texture throughout Truckin→Other One.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill's kick drum gets special mention for added ‘umph’; drumming supports the tight, driving feel of the set.
